headline: February inflation data, earnings, Fed commentary: What to Watch
summary: Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton takes a look at the top stories for investors to watch on Wednesday, March 11. February's Consumer Price Index (CPI) data will be out in the morning, offering the latest insight into the US economy. Campbell's (CPB), Petco (WOOF), and more are reporting quarterly earnings results. Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman will deliver remarks. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

headline: Retail layoffs hit 150-year-old food company’s Texas plant
summary: Campbell’s, the 150-year-old packaged food company known for its iconic canned soups, is cutting jobs at a Texas manufacturing facility as it restructures operations, amid growing pressure across the retail sector. headline: Campbell’s Earnings Are Coming. Its Place in the S&P 500 Is at Stake.
summary: The S&P 500 is designed to represent the largest and most liquid companies. Campbell’s is now among the smallest companies in the index.

headline: Should Campbell’s Elevated Supply Chain Focus Under Cassandra Green Shift the Margin Story for CPB?
summary: Campbell’s recently appointed longtime executive Cassandra Green as Chief Supply Chain Officer, expanding her remit to include end-to-end logistics, procurement, manufacturing, and now food safety and quality under CEO Mick Beekhuizen. This move elevates supply chain execution as a core focus at a time when the company is contending with cost pressures, competition, and cautious investor sentiment around earnings. Next, we’ll examine how Green’s expanded supply chain role could influence...

headline: Is Campbell Soup (CPB) Now An Opportunity After A 36% One-Year Share Price Slide?
summary: If you are wondering whether Campbell's current share price reflects its true worth, this article walks through what the numbers are saying about value right now. The stock closed at US$25.29, with returns of a 3.9% decline over 7 days, a 12.3% decline over 30 days, an 8.7% decline year to date and a 36.8% decline over 1 year, which will catch the eye of anyone weighing up potential upside against recent weakness. Recent coverage has focused on Campbell's position as a branded food producer...

headline: Oracle Earnings, G-7 Meeting, Inflation Data: What to Watch This Week
summary: Oracle headlines an earnings calendar that also features Adobe and Dollar General. The surge in energy prices may fuel extra interest in the two inflation readings on the slate. And the Senate is expected to vote as soon as this week on a housing bill that would force large investors to sell homes.
